gmd:metadataStandardName.gco:CharacterString: ISO 19115-2 Geographic Information - Metadata Part 2 Extensions for imagery and gridded data

gmd:title.gco:CharacterString: Electronic Monitoring in the Pacific Islands Longline Fisheries Data

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: View the complete metadata record on InPort for more information about this dataset.

gmd:abstract.gco:CharacterString: Electronic monitoring (EM) data are collected aboard Hawaii longline vessels (shallow- and deep-set) participating in a pre-implementation voluntary program. These data include video collected during hauling of fishing gear and sensor data collected throughout the fishing trip. Selected EM trips are reviewed for the detection of catch and bycatch species with identification performed to species or species groups.

gmd:purpose.gco:CharacterString: To determine the efficacy of EM in the Hawaii and American Samoa longline fisheries as a monitoring tool.

gmd:maintenanceNote.gco:CharacterString: Data are finalized after each research project is completed

gmd:keyword.gco:CharacterString: DOC/NOAA/NMFS/PIFSC > Pacific Islands Fisheries Science Center, National Marine Fisheries Service, NOAA, U.S. Department of Commerce

gmd:title.gco:CharacterString: Global Change Master Directory (GCMD) Data Center Keywords

gmd:organisationName.gco:CharacterString: Earth Science Data and Information System, Earth Science Projects Division, Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC), National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: Global Change Master Directory (GCMD). 2025. GCMD Keywords, Version 22. Greenbelt, MD: Earth Science Data and Information System, Earth Science Projects Division, Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC), National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA). URL (GCMD Keyword Forum Page): https://forum.earthdata.nasa.gov/app.php/tag/GCMD+Keywords

gmd:keyword.gco:CharacterString: Electronic Monitoring in PI Longline Fisheries

gmd:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: Cite As: Pacific Islands Fisheries Science Center, [Date of Access]: Electronic Monitoring in the Pacific Islands Longline Fisheries Data [Data Date Range], https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/inport/item/62654.

gmd:useLimitation.gco:CharacterString: NOAA provides no warranty, nor accepts any liability occurring from any incomplete, incorrect, or misleading data, or from any incorrect, incomplete, or misleading use of the data. It is the responsibility of the user to determine whether or not the data is suitable for the intended purpose.

gmd:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: Access Constraints: These are confidential data and may not be made available in unaggregated form without proper confidentiality documents on file with FRMD Division.

Distribution Liability: The user is responsible for the results of any application of this data for other than its intended purpose. No warranty expressed or implied is made regarding the accuracy utility of the data, nor shall the act of distribution constitute any such warranty. This disclaimer applies both to individual use of the data and aggregate use with other data. It is strongly recommended that careful attention be paid to the contents of the metadata file associated with the data. PIFSC shall not be held liable for improper or incorrect use of the data described or contained herein.

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Data Management Plan for this record on InPort.

gmd:code.gco:CharacterString: HIGH SEAS FISHING IN THE PACIFIC

gmd:evaluationMethodDescription.gco:CharacterString: EM data for review are selected from shallow- or deep-set longline trips from vessels participating in the voluntary program.Â

Discarded catch may remain undetected if caught and released outside the camera field of view (e.g. sharks and seabirds). Handling methods may vary between fishing crews and vessels and may result in variability in detection, especially for discarded catch. In addition, video review speed and reviewer attention or experience may bias the detection and/or identification of retained or discarded catch.Â

Species detections and identifications are comparable to fisheries observer data for retained catch. However, EM currently underreports discarded catch compared to fisheries observer data. A catch handling study will begin in 2021 to improve discard detection with EM.

gmd:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: Access Constraints: None | Use Constraints: None
